ocr.content:
Dmitri Alperovitch
Bears in the Midst: Intrusion into the Democratic National Committee
crowdstrike.

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis:
The analyzed document is a standard PDF file presenting a technical threat intelligence report by CrowdStrike regarding a historical cyber intrusion. Internal structural analysis reveals no malicious anomalies, automated execution chains, embedded executables, or JavaScript. The file consists of standard structural elements, including several embedded hyperlinks and correctly encoded text and image streams.

Visually, the document demonstrates professional formatting, authentic branding, and an analytical tone consistent with a legitimate cybersecurity white paper. While the text lists various indicators of compromise, such as malicious IP addresses and file hashes, these are presented strictly for defensive and educational purposes. There are no phishing lures, credential-harvesting forms, or deceptive urgency cues present, confirming the document is harmless.

ocr.content:
May 25, 2017
Tainted Leaks Disinformation and Phishing With a Russian
Nexus
citizenlab.ca
/2017/05/t

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature detects a PDF file that contains JavaScript. JavaScript can be used to customize PDFs by implementing objects, methods, and properties. While not inherently malicious, embedding JavaScript inside of a PDF is often used for malicious purposes such as malware delivery or exploitation.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis:
The document is a legitimate research report from the cybersecurity organization Citizen Lab, titled "Tainted Leaks Disinformation and Phishing With a Russian Nexus." The visual content is professional, academic, and lacks any red flags associated with malicious documents, such as urgency cues or impersonation. Its purpose is to analyze a state-sponsored phishing campaign, not to conduct one.

The internal technical analysis corroborates this assessment. Although it identified a large number of URIs (140) and numerous stream decoding errors, it found no evidence of a viable execution chain. Key malicious components, such as automatic action triggers (`/OpenAction`) or embedded JavaScript, are absent. The presence of many URLs is consistent with the document's nature as a research paper citing evidence.

In conclusion, the file is a static, informational document. The technical anomalies are not linked to any malicious functionality, and the visual context confirms the document's benign nature as a public security report.

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature fires on the presence of Base64 encoded URI prefixes (http:// and https://) across any file. The simple presence of such strings is not inherently an indicator of malicious content, but is worth further investigation.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature identifies Adobe Extensible Metadata Platform (XMP) identifiers embedded within files. Defined as a standard for mapping graphical asset relationships, XMP allows for tracking of both parent-child relationships and individual revisions. There are three categories of identifiers: original document, document, and instance. Generally, XMP data is stored in XML format, updated on save/copy, and embedded within the graphical asset. These identifiers can be used to track both malicious and benign graphics within common Microsoft and Adobe document lures.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

attributes.meaningful_name: Border and Rule by Harsha Walia [Harsha Walia] (z-lib.org).pdf

crowdsourced_yara_results.description: This signature identifies Adobe Extensible Metadata Platform (XMP) identifiers embedded within files. Defined as a standard for mapping graphical asset relationships, XMP allows for tracking of both parent-child relationships and individual revisions. There are three categories of identifiers: original document, document, and instance. Generally, XMP data is stored in XML format, updated on save/copy, and embedded within the graphical asset. These identifiers can be used to track both malicious and benign graphics within common Microsoft and Adobe document lures.
crowdsourced_yara_results.author: InQuest Labs

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis:
The document is identified as a legitimate digital copy or promotional excerpt of the book "Border and Rule" by Harsha Walia, published by Haymarket Books. Visual and text analysis confirms the presence of consistent, legitimate branding and standard promotional endorsements. The document does not contain any interactive forms, credential harvesting mechanisms, or phishing-related urgency cues. The only visible URL routes to the legitimate publisher's domain.

Although the internal technical analysis failed to complete due to a timeout, this is expected behavior when processing a large, text-heavy document of nearly 400 pages and is not indicative of a threat. The high volume of URLs and phone numbers referenced in the document's metadata is typical for a scholarly text that relies heavily on footnotes, references, and academic citations. No malicious or suspicious elements were detected.

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis:
The document is assessed as benign. The visual analysis confirms its identity as a legitimate academic publication, "OrizzonteCina," from the Italian institute "Istituto Affari Internazionali." All visual elements, including branding, logos, and the provided contact email, are authentic and consistent. The content is professional and informational, containing no phishing lures, urgency cues, or other social engineering tactics.

The internal technical analysis supports this conclusion. While the file contains a JavaScript object and numerous URIs, the investigation found no viable execution chain or automatic trigger to activate them. The presence of these internal components, without a mechanism for execution or any corresponding visual deception, does not constitute a threat. Therefore, the document is considered a safe, non-interactive publication.
